item.title: Dollar stays stable after Trump says Iran war could finish soon
item.description: The dollar was largely steady вҖӢon Wednesday, with gains from safe-haven demand fading, as U.S. President Donald Trump said the war with вҖҢIran could wind down within weeks, though conflicting signals on the conflict kept investors on edge.

item.title: Front-month Brent oil futures extend gains after record monthly rise in March
item.description: Oil prices ticked up in early Wednesday trade, with Brent front-month futures extending a record March rally as Middle East volatility kept markets jittery, despite reports that the вҖӢU.S. and Iran may be edging closer to a negotiated end to the war.

item.title: Apple's 50-year journey from garage to tech titan
item.description: In early 1976 in California, Steve Wozniak had just completed the design of a computer circuit board he intended to share with fellow hobbyists at a prominent local club. His friend Steve Jobs also saw a business opportunity вҖӢto manufacture and sell the boards, and thus Apple was born.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lines up 21 banks for mega IPO, code-named project Apex
item.description: SpaceX is working with at least 21 banks on its blockbuster initial public offering, people familiar вҖӢwith the matter said on Tuesday, one of the largest underwriting вҖҢsyndicates assembled in recent years.

item.title: New Zealand's Fonterra admits 'grass-fed' label may mislead shoppers after Greenpeace lawsuit
item.description: New Zealand's Fonterra said on Wednesday it had settled a lawsuit filed by environmental group Greenpeace by вҖӢadmitting the label on one of its products вҖҢwas likely to mislead some shoppers about what its cows were actually fed.

item.title: Microsoft, Chevron and Engine No. 1 sign exclusive deal for power supply
item.description: Microsoft , Chevron and investment fund Engine No. 1 have entered into an exclusivity agreement for power вҖӢgeneration and supply, the three companies said on Tuesday.

item.title: Insider trading in prediction markets is among CFTC priorities, top cop says
item.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission will focus on policing market вҖӢmisconduct such as insider trading in prediction markets and market вҖҢmanipulation in energy markets, the agency's new enforcement director said on Tuesday.

item.title: Republican senator proposes to widen US ban on Chinese autos
item.description: Republican Senator Bernie Moreno said on Tuesday he will introduce legislation next month to toughen a U.S. government ban on Chinese automakers from entering the American market, and encouraged other countries to вҖӢfollow suit.

item.title: Anthropic to sign deal with Australia on AI safety and economic data tracking
item.description: Anthropic said on Wednesday it would sign an agreement to share its economic index data вҖӢwith the Australian government to help track вҖҢartificial intelligence adoption across the economy, and its impact on workers and jobs.

item.title: AT&T signs deal worth $2 billion to upgrade emergency cellular network
item.description: AT&T has reached a deal to invest about $1 billion to вҖӢimprove the Commerce Department's FirstNet вҖҢand deliver $1 billion in cost savings for the program via reduced rates, a U.S вҖӢgovernment agency said on Tuesday.

item.title: Exclusive: Investor Artisan Partners backs Unilever's plan to sell food unit
item.description: Artisan Partners, вҖӢwhich has a history of driving strategic change at consumer companies, welcomed Unilever's decision on Tuesday to merge its вҖҢfood business with McCormick , saying the Dove soap maker should now be able to "more effectively manage" its core personal care and home brands.

item.title: X down for thousands of users in the US, Downdetector shows
item.description: Social media platform вҖӢX was down вҖҢfor thousands of users вҖӢin вҖӢthe U.S. on Tuesday, вҒ according вҖӢto Downdetector.com.

item.title: Trading Day: Q1 ends with a BANG!
item.description: Wall Street closed a tumultuous first quarter with a bang on Tuesday as hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East powered stocks to their best day in almost a year, even as data showed a slump in U.S. job openings and hirings.

item.title: Beyond Meat misses quarterly results estimates on persistent demand struggles
item.description: Faux meat maker Beyond Meat missed Wall Street estimates for fourth-quarter sales and posted a larger-than-expected operating loss on Tuesday due вҖӢto sluggish demand for its higher-priced processed food products.

item.title: NY Fed appoints Citi CEO Jane Fraser to Federal Advisory Council
item.description: The Federal Reserve Bank of New вҖӢYork said on Tuesday it has appointed Jane вҖҢFraser, chief executive of Citigroup , to the Federal Reserve Board of Governors' Advisory Council.

item.title: Elon Musk must face class action over lateВ disclosure of Twitter stake, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday said former Twitter investors вҖҢwho accused Elon Musk of defrauding them by waiting too long to disclose his initial investment in the social media company may pursue their case as a class action.

item.title: North Korea-linked hack hits largely invisible software that powers online services
item.description: Hackers linked to North Korea breached behind-the-scenes software that runs many common online functions in an effort to steal login information that could enable further cyber operations, вҖӢGoogle said on Tuesday.

item.title: Nike results top estimates as turnaround shows uneven progress
item.description: Nike beat expectations for third-quarter earnings on Tuesday, as the sportswear giant's turnaround efforts showed some signs of progress, particularly in вҖӢits wholesale business, even as weakness in China and margin pressure вҖҢpersisted.

item.title: MercadoLibre's fintech terminates its cryptocurrency Mercado Coin
item.description: Mercado Pago, the fintech arm of e-commerce вҖӢfirm MercadoLibre , said on Tuesday вҖҢit is discontinuing its cryptocurrency, called Mercado Coin, which it had launched вҖӢin 2022 as part of вҖӢits loyalty program.

item.title: US customs agency says tariff refund system progressing, but payments may take up to 45 days
item.description: The U.S. customs agency said on Tuesday it was making progress in setting up a streamlined process for refunding some $166 billion in tariff collections deemed illegal вҖӢby the Supreme Court, but that its new system could take up to вҖҢ45 days to review and process refund applications.

item.title: Monzo to close US business to focus on UK and Europe
item.description: British вҖҢmobile bank Monzo said on Tuesday it is exiting the United States, describing вҖӢthe move as a strategic вҖӢdecision to focus on expanding in вҒ the UK and Europe.

item.title: Exclusive: TikTok seeks Brazil fintech license to offer credit
item.description: Social media platform TikTok, controlled by China's ByteDance, is seeking approval from the Brazilian central bank to operate as a lending and вҖӢpayments fintech, according to two people with direct knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Oracle begins cutting thousands of jobs, CNBC reports
item.description: Cloud computing company Oracle is laying off thousands of employees, CNBC reported on Tuesday, citing two вҖӢpeople familiar with the matter.

item.title: German automaker Mercedes-Benz to invest $4 billion in Alabama SUV plant
item.description: German automaker Mercedes-Benz said on Tuesday it вҖӢwill invest $4 billion at its Alabama plant вҖҢthrough 2030 to boost SUV production as it seeks to address significant U.S. auto tariffs.

item.title: Unilever and McCormick investors find $65 billion food deal hard to swallow
item.description: Unilever and McCormick shareholders found the reality of their planned $65 billion вҖӢfood deal hard to digest on Tuesday, perturbed by the transaction's structure, its long timeline to closing and the potential for antitrust scrutiny.

item.title: Activist investor Irenic swoops in on Snap with new stake, shares surge
item.description: Activist investor Irenic Capital Management said on Tuesday that technology company Snap could вҖӢbe worth at least five times as much as today's valuation but вҖҢneeds to cut costs, optimize its portfolio and use artificial intelligence better.

item.title: Apple tests Siri feature that handles multiple commands at once, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Apple is testing a вҖӢfeature that would let Siri process multiple requests in вҖҢa single query, bringing the virtual assistant more in line with the capabilities of newer AI assistants, Bloomberg News reported on вҖӢTuesday.

item.title: WTO cannot address trade imbalances, but US not ready to exit, Greer says
item.description: The World Trade Organization is unable to deal with the core challenges of the global trading system, but the вҖӢU.S. is not ready to leave the body, U.S. Trade вҖҢRepresentative Jamieson Greer said on Tuesday.

item.title: KKR plans to privatise Japan's Taiyo Holdings with $3.3 billion tender offer
item.description: Global investment firm KKR said вҖӢon Tuesday that an entity owned вҖҢby its investment funds seeks to make a tender offer to buy all shares of Japanese chemical вҖӢmanufacturer Taiyo Holdings for 528.56 billion вҖӢyen ($3.33 billion).

item.title: Warren Buffett will not commit to more donations to Gates Foundation
item.description: Warren Buffett on Tuesday declined to commit to continuing his annual вҖӢmultibillion-dollar donations to the Gates Foundation, following the recent release of materials about late sex offender вҖҢJeffrey Epstein's relationship with the nonprofit and its co-founder Bill Gates.
